title: nolana leptophylla subsp. mollis
intro: Nolana leptophylla subsp. mollis, also known as Chilean bush or Chilean bush flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat bacterial infections. The health benefits of Nolana leptophylla subsp. mollis include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and its potential to help reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Nolana leptophylla subsp. mollis can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water or oil, and are often used topically to promote healing and relaxation.
